one_time_description: Gain on disposal of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: First Nation operates a gas bar, fish sales, sawmill trust (Asiniika Wiikwedong Sawmill Trust), forestry operations (OFNLP2008, BZA Forestry, Lake Nipigon Forest Management), and business development through Biinjitiwaabik Zaaging Anishinaabek Development Inc. COVID-19 impacted operations including band office closure and increased member support demands.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) budgets were not approved as required under PSAS; (2) scope limitation on prior year gas bar sales of $802,196 and inventory for resale of $41,705 at March 31, 2020 due to COVID-19 restrictions affecting the carry-forward into 2021 figures. Five entities consolidated: Amik Business Trust, Amik Forest Renewal Trust, Asiniika Wiikwedong Sawmill Trust, Gi-Niijaasinaanig Obimadiziwinowaa Community Corporation, and Biinjitiwaabik Zaaging Anishinaabek Development Inc. Tangible capital asset net book value not extractable from text provided (Schedule 1 not fully included). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,237,400 + FNIH $425,425 + CMHC $98,070 + Ministry of Indigenous Affairs $237,212 + Ministry of Children Community and Social Services $1,273,939 + Ministry of Health $135,370 + KEB $2,347,855 + Union of Ontario Indians $426,731 = $9,182,002 minus admin recovery and OSR items; OSR includes gas bar, fish sales, interest, admin recovery, AETS, Dilico, Lake Nipigon Forest, OFNLP2008, rental, other revenue. Financial statements signed February 28, 2023 for March 31, 2021 year-end indicating significant delay in preparation.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
